So he asked me to look at it and he had been asking me to look at it and try things out. And eventually he realized, I'm pretty good at this because I have a knack of seeing things from different angles and trying things different ways. And he ultimately wound up having me do the QA portion. He would send a bill for his software development stuff, but then there would be a QA invoice as well. And that was just the work I did. It was never officially under my name. And that's how I got into QA. I taught myself how to effectively look at every aspect of the software from both a usability and a practicality standpoint.
